name: Launching Next
description: A long-lived directory of hand-picked new and upcoming startups. The audience is early adopters, journalists and investors browsing for fresh products, and listings remain discoverable for the long term.

auth.notes: No account required for the standard listing — you fill a public submission form and provide your email. A paid express/featured option skips the review queue and adds placement.

best_practices: Optimize for longevity — the listing stays up for years, so write a description that won't feel dated and avoid time-sensitive phrasing.
Fill out the long-form fields completely; Launching Next asks for both a short and a long description, and richer entries get approved and surfaced more.
Make the short description keyword-rich but readable, since it doubles as discovery/SEO text in the directory.
Choose the most accurate category so the right browsers and the topic pages find you.
Provide a working website and a Twitter handle; complete, verifiable links speed up manual review.
Use a clear product name and a square logo that reads well at small sizes in list view.

questions.label: Short description
questions.help: One or two sentences summarizing the product; used in listings and search.

questions.label: Full description
questions.help: A fuller explanation of what it does, the problem, and who it's for.
